description: This micrograph presents a metallographic cross-section of the duplex Fe–20Mn–6Al alloy following isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hr in pure oxygen at 200 torr. The image reveals a stratified scale morphology characteristic of Region II behavior: bulky manganese-rich oxides dominate the upper dark scale layer, while a light-etching ferrite surface layer lies directly beneath the oxide–metal interface, indicating austenite depletion in the substrate immediately below the scale. The underlying metal microstructure shows clearly resolved grain and phase boundaries. The 20 μm scale bar confirms the micron-scale dimensions of the ferrite layer and oxide protrusions. As reported in the accompanying text, this morphology exemplifies how austenite is detrimental to oxidation resistance in Fe–Mn–Al alloys, promoting breakdown of preexisting alumina scales and growth of bulky manganese-rich oxides above secondary ferrite layers that form during high-temperature exposure.
